question: What type of meld is this?
description: Three identical Bamboo 7 tiles. A meld is a set of tiles that counts toward your winning hand.
explanation: This is a Pong — 3 identical tiles. A Kong needs 4 identical tiles. A Chow needs 3 consecutive numbers in the same suit.

question: What tile completes this winning hand?
description: This hand has 5 complete melds (15 tiles) and one 1-Circle tile. A winning hand = 5 melds + 1 pair. What tile completes the pair?
explanation: You have 5 complete melds already. To win (Hu), you need 5 melds + 1 pair (17 tiles). You have one 1-Circle, so drawing another 1-Circle completes your pair and wins the hand.

question: What's your best move?
description: The player ahead discarded a 5-Character. You already have three 5-Character tiles in your hand.
explanation: You have 3 tiles already — adding the discarded 4th makes a Kong (4 identical tiles). You can't Pong because that's only 3. You can't Chow because 5-Character has no consecutive partners shown here.
